chapter_goal: Interpret Willmore surfaces through energy variants, Euler-Lagrange residuals, elastic-cylinder reduction, and inversion invariance.
computational_checks: exact torus energy minimum at R/r=sqrt(2)
Gauss-Bonnet offsets for genus 0 and 1 examples
artifact existence and nonblank image statistics
concept_inventory: W(f)=integral H^2 dA as the surface analog of squared curvature energy
E(f)=integral(H^2-K/2)dA and W_hat(f)=integral(H^2-K)dA differ from W by Gaussian-curvature terms
Gauss-Bonnet turns those differences into genus offsets for closed surfaces
Willmore surfaces satisfy Delta H + 2H(H^2-K)=0 away from boundary terms
cylinders over plane curves reduce the Willmore equation to kappa'' + kappa^3/2 = 0
inversion rescales metric and area while preserving the conformal density (H^2-K)dA

library_routing_table.why: static comparisons and exact genus-offset algebra are most readable here

library_routing_table.representation: parameterized surface grids colored by curvature density
library_routing_table.why: small analytic grids keep curvature formulas visible
the learner needs to rotate both surfaces and inspect the shared density scale
the source proof chain is structural rather than decorative

risks: mesh-free parameter grids avoid new dependencies but do not approximate arbitrary Willmore surfaces
inversion checks avoid the center singularity by translating the sampled torus away from the origin
source figures are not copied; all visuals are generated from formulas in this notebook

source_span_read.source_map_focus: Willmore functional, Willmore equation, energy offsets, cylinder examples, and inversion invariance.

visual_sequence.validation: pointwise density error below tolerance away from the inversion center
